name: 3Min 34/50 Cross
full_name: 3-Minute EMA 34/50 Cross with Daily BX Bias

indicators.BX: RSI(EMA(close, 5) - EMA(close, 20), 5) - 50 — oscillates around 0. Positive = bullish momentum, negative = bearish.

current_rules.bias: Daily BX. BX > 0 = BULLISH (longs only). BX < 0 = BEARISH (shorts only). Use prior completed daily bar — no lookahead.
current_rules.entry: 3min EMA34 crosses EMA50 in the bias direction. Long: EMA34 crosses above EMA50 when daily BX > 0. Short: EMA34 crosses below EMA50 when daily BX < 0.
current_rules.exit: Opposite 3min EMA34/50 cross. No other exit condition.
current_rules.session: All hours (CME Globex). Session filter TBD after V1 results.
current_rules.sizing: % move on close price. No options modeling in V1.

versions.name: Baseline
versions.notes: First run. All hours, no session filter, no additional conditions. GC and CL.

hypothesis: The 3min EMA34/50 cross fires at the beginning of directional moves. Filtering by daily BX (momentum-normalized bias) removes cross noise in choppy/ranging days and keeps only moves aligned with the prevailing daily trend.
open_questions: Does daily BX filter actually improve win rate vs no filter?
What is the optimal session window — all hours vs RTH only?
GC vs CL — does one behave better with this approach?
How many trades per year? Is it tradeable frequency?
